title: Trachoma papuanum
intro: Trachoma papuanum, also known as the Papuan trachoma fern or Papuan hair fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae, native to tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is used to treat a range of ailments, including respiratory issues, fever, and skin conditions. Trachoma papuanum contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to soothe coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Trachoma papuanum is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to Australia, specifically the Northern Territory, Queensland, and Western Australia. Trachoma papuanum is a shrub that grows up to 1.5 meters in height and is often found in dry, sandy soils. The plant has white or purple flowers that are tubular in shape and attract various insects for pollination. The classification of Trachoma papuanum places it within the genus Goodenia.
